title: Emberdeep Mine
blurb: A lost dwarven mine, a kobold warband, and the young red dragon nesting in its flooded forge.

mine_mouth.scene: Heat breathes up out of the mountain. The old dwarven mine of Emberdeep was lost to a kobold horde a season ago, and now an orange glow pulses somewhere far below — the warband, the rumors say, serves a young red dragon nesting in the flooded forge at the mine's heart. A dying prospector slumps against a cart, pressing a flask of strong draught into your hands and rasping a warning: 'The lower gallery's drowned. There's a sluice — drain it, or take the cave-in drift, and pray.' A single timber-shored adit leads down into the dark.

kobold_warren.scene: A cavern strung with bone-charms and salvaged lanterns. Two kobold sentries skitter atop a rickety scaffold of mine-props, not yet certain what moved in the dark below them. A great rusted sluice-wheel juts from the wall — heave it and the flooded lower gallery would drain. Otherwise the only other way down is a half-collapsed drift, black and groaning. Loose planks sag underfoot everywhere.

the drained passage into the flooded gal.denied: Black water still fills the passage to the chest — there is no wading it until the sluice is heaved open and the gallery drains.

flooded_gallery.scene: The sluice has done its work: the gallery is a shin-deep slick of mud and dead fish, the air thick and sour. A cracked methane pocket hisses from a fissure in the ceiling — one stray flame or hard knock could touch it off. Half-sunk in the silt, a Pearl of Power glints in a drowned dwarf's strongbox. The forge-glow throbs through an archway ahead.

collapsed_drift.scene: A throat of broken stone, barely shoulder-wide, that shudders with every step. A pair of kobolds have made a killing-ground of it, perched where the ceiling sags lowest, ready to bring the roof down on intruders. It is the short road to the forge, and the cruel one.

forge_heart.scene: The mountain's old forge-cavern, walls running with molten light. Coiled atop a glittering scree of dwarven gold and ash sleeps — no longer — Cinderfang, a young red dragon, smoke curling from its nostrils as one great amber eye cracks open. There is nowhere left to go but through it. Behind the dragon, half-melted into the hoard, lies the lost Emberdeep Crown.
